steps.title: Identify the contrast between Guy-Blaché's approach and period norms
steps.content: Guy-Blaché encouraged actors to behave naturally, but the passage explains that silent films required actors to exaggerate their actions and expressions. This gap between her vision and industry practice is the key signal.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to find the best completion
steps.content:
Answer C directly fits the contrast: if silent films made exaggeration the norm, then encouraging natural acting made Guy-Blaché's approach very unusual for the period.

The other three choices either claim her style was copied or familiar to actors (contradicting the exaggeration norm), or make claims unsupported by the passage.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
